title: Linosyris refracta
intro: Linosyris refracta, also known as White Horehound, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Linosyris refracta include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, White Horehound is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Linosyris refracta is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Linosyris, which is a part of the Asteraceae family. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. Linosyris refracta is commonly classified as a shrubby or herbaceous plant. Its classification as a member of the Asteraceae family indicates its relationship to daisies and other related flowering plants.
uses: Linosyris refracta is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. In some cultures, Linosyris refracta is used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential side effects.
